# Mainstreet IQ

> Mainstreet IQ is a fractional and interim CFO services firm led by Scott Hess, providing embedded finance leadership for growing companies between $15M and $100M in revenue. Based in California, the firm works across e-commerce, SaaS, manufacturing, hospitality, apparel/DTC, and PE-backed companies. Every engagement is built on the proprietary RAID Framework (Redundant, Agile, Intelligent, Data-Driven).

## Core Services

- [Fractional CFO — Ongoing](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-services): 10–15 hours/week embedded finance leadership (fractional or interim CFO) covering month-end close, cash/margin visibility, investor reporting, and cross-functional strategy.
- [Advisory — Light Touch](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-services): ~10 hours/month strategic oversight for companies with existing finance teams.
- [Project-Based](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-services): Discrete engagements — financial modeling, M&A preparation, close process redesign, investor reporting packages.

## About

- [About Scott Hess](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/about): 20+ years CFO experience. Former CFO at Watch Gang ($40M subscription ecommerce) and Haberdash (luxury menswear). 12 years at Northern Trust in institutional finance. Navy veteran. MBA from Pepperdine Graziadio School of Business.

## Case Studies

- [E-Commerce: $15M Company](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-work/e-commercegrowth): Scaling finance through hypergrowth — inventory forecasting, margin analysis by channel, KPI dashboards, enterprise accounting migration.
- [Manufacturing: $300M Divestiture](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-work/divesting-manufacturer): Financial carve-out, standalone financials, data room construction, deal support for business unit sale.
- [Apparel/DTC: $10M Brand](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-work/designer-led-apparel): Product costing, seasonal cash flow forecasting, channel profitability analysis, working capital optimization.
- [Hospitality: Six-Location Restaurant Group](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-work/restaurant-group): Unified multi-location reporting, food/labor cost controls, expansion modeling for coastal California restaurants.
- [Turnaround: Asset Purchase](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-work/asset-purchase): Management-led acquisition from bankruptcy — valuation, financing, 100-day integration, finance function built from scratch.
- [SaaS: Post-Seed Series A Prep](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-work/saas-post-seed-funding): SaaS metrics implementation (ARR, MRR, CAC, LTV, churn), cohort analysis, revenue recognition, investor-ready reporting.
- [Family Office](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/our-work/family-office): Unified reporting across PE, real estate, operating businesses. Institutional-grade investment analysis and multigenerational governance.

## The RAID Framework

Every engagement is structured around four principles:
- **Redundant**: Documented, cross-trained processes — no single points of failure.
- **Agile**: Automation-first — build systems before hiring people.
- **Intelligent**: Finance teams as owner-operators, not scorekeepers.
- **Data-Driven**: Every team member owns 3–5 KPIs tied to real business levers.

- [Book a 30-Minute Call](https://calendly.com/scott_mainstreetiq/30min): Free initial consultation.

## Blog — Finance Insights (31 posts)

### The RAID Framework Series (8 parts)
- [Why Most Finance Teams Fail to Scale](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/why-finance-teams-fail-to-scale): The pattern of finance breaking at $20M — and the 4 failure modes. Series opener.
- [Automate First — Why Tools Come Before People](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/automate-first): The "Agile" principle — automation before headcount. AP, AR, expense, and reconciliation automation.
- [Build Redundant Finance — No More Single Points of Failure](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/build-redundant-finance): The "Redundant" principle — documentation, cross-training, and system access redundancy.
- [Stay Agile — Shrink the Team, Grow the Revenue](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/stay-agile): Organizational agility — why a $25M company needs 3 finance people, not 8.
- [Intelligent Teams Think Like Owners](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/intelligent-teams): The "Intelligent" principle — scorekeepers vs. owner-operators. Building teams that drive decisions.
- [Moneyball Metrics — The 3–5 KPIs Each Role Must Own](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/moneyball-metrics): The "Data-Driven" principle — stage-based KPI selection and ownership by role.
- [RAID Tech Stack — Tools That Reduce FTEs](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/raid-tech-stack): The specific tools — QBO vs NetSuite, Bill.com, Ramp, FloQast, Mosaic.
- [Investor-Ready Finance — Fewer FTE, More Control](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/investor-ready-finance): The 5 things investors look for in your finance function. Series conclusion.

### RAID in Action
- [RAID in Action](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/raid-in-action): Real-world case study of the RAID Framework applied to a growing company — the problems, the fixes, and the results.

### Annual Planning Series (6 parts)
- [Why an Annual Plan Matters](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/why-annual-plan-matters): The real value of annual planning isn't the document — it's forcing alignment between revenue, expenses, headcount, and cash flow.
- [Revenue Growth Planning](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/revenue-growth-planning): Building bottoms-up revenue models by channel with unit economics, capacity constraints, and scenario analysis.
- [Expense Controls](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/expense-controls): Separating fixed from variable, benchmarking against revenue percentages, and building approval workflows that catch bad spending early.
- [Marketing Spend Planning](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/marketing-spend-planning): Tying marketing spend to CAC targets by channel, setting payback period guardrails, and giving marketing a flexible budget framework.
- [Balance Sheet Management](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/balance-sheet-management): Managing working capital, inventory, and receivables proactively — the balance sheet tells you what the P&L can't.
- [Planning for Unknowns](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/planning-for-unknowns): Scenario planning, contingency reserves, and monthly reforecasting that turn a rigid plan into a flexible operating framework.

### Acquisition & Growth Series (5 parts)
- [If Your Month-End Close Takes More Than 7 Days](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/month-end-close): How to go from an 18-day close to a 5-day close in 90 days.
- [Your Marketing Team Might Be Destroying Your Margin](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/marketing-team-destroying-margin): The gap between marketing efficiency (MER) and actual profitability lives in COGS assumptions, shipping costs, and return rates.
- [Tariffs and Lower Middle Market COGS](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/tariffs-lower-middle-market-cogs): How tariffs cascade through pricing, margins, inventory strategy, and cash flow — and how to model for them.
- [Your Comp Structure Is Driving the Wrong Behavior](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/comp-structure-wrong-behavior): When compensation plans don't align with margin, cash collection, and retention, you're paying people to optimize the wrong things.
- [The Payback Window Problem](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/payback-window-problem): Why most companies calculate payback period wrong — and how getting it right changes which channels you scale.

### Finance Leadership & Operations
- [The Cash Flow Illusion](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/the-cash-flow-illusion): How cash hides in inventory, accounts receivable, and expense timing — and the 13-week forecast that fixes it.
- [MER Looks Healthy, Margin Is Disappearing](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/mer-margin-disappearing): Marketing efficiency ratio vs. contribution margin — why the metric everyone watches isn't the one that matters.
- [CFO vs. Finance Director vs. Controller](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/cfo-finance-director-controller): What each role actually owns, which one fits your revenue stage, and how to avoid the expensive hiring mistake.
- [How to Evaluate a Fractional CFO](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/evaluate-fractional-cfo): What to look for when hiring a fractional CFO — industry experience, engagement model, and red flags.
- [Your Finance Team Stopped Scaling](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/finance-team-stopped-scaling): Five signs your finance function has stalled — and why the fix is structural, not tactical.
- [The Hero Problem Is a Liability](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/hero-problem-liability): When one person holds all the keys to your finance function, you don't have a team — you have a single point of failure.
- [The Marketing Problem Is a Merchandising Problem](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/marketing-problem-merchandising): When revenue underperforms, the instinct is to blame marketing — but the root cause is often product mix and merchandising.
- [Running a Business With a Better CFO](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/running-business-better-cfo): What changes when you bring in senior finance leadership that understands the business, not just the books.
- [What Your CAC Number Doesn't Include](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/what-cac-number-doesnt-include): The hidden costs that inflate your real customer acquisition cost beyond what the marketing dashboard reports.
- [The Finance Side of a PE Exit](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/pe-exit-finance-side): What gets tested in a quality of earnings process and how to prepare your finance function before the LOI.
- [What PE Sponsors Actually Ask About EBITDA](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/blog/pe-sponsors-ebitda): The specific EBITDA questions PE firms ask in diligence — and what "can answer them cleanly" actually means.

## Service Areas

- [Santa Barbara County](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/fractional-cfo-santa-barbara): Fractional and interim CFO services for Santa Barbara County — wine, tourism, tech, agriculture.
- [San Luis Obispo County](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/fractional-cfo-san-luis-obispo): Fractional and interim CFO services for SLO County — Paso Robles wine, agriculture, Cal Poly tech pipeline.
- [Ventura County](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/fractional-cfo-ventura): Fractional and interim CFO services for Ventura County — agriculture, defense, manufacturing, biotech.
- [Los Angeles](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/fractional-cfo-los-angeles): Fractional and interim CFO services for LA — entertainment, DTC/ecommerce, apparel, PE-backed companies.
- [Orange County](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/fractional-cfo-orange-county): Fractional and interim CFO services for Orange County — tech/SaaS, ecommerce, medical devices, franchises.

## Optional

- [Central Coast IQ](https://www.mainstreetiq.com/central-coast-iq): Wine country intelligence and back office operations for owner-operator wineries on California's Central Coast.
